meta.title: Singapore RWA Ecosystem Map
meta.description: 6-layer structured overview: Regulatory → Institutions → Infrastructure → Issuance → Market Access → Enablers
meta.disclaimer: This map is compiled from publicly available official sources. Participant lists may be incomplete where information has not been publicly enumerated by regulators. This is an informational reference, not a regulatory register.
sources: MAS Payment Services Act licensing register (mas.gov.sg)
Company public filings and press releases

layers.label: Regulatory Oversight
layers.description: Primary regulators governing tokenized assets, payment services, and digital assets in Singapore under the Payment Services Act and Securities and Futures Act.

participants.name: MAS
participants.role: Central bank and financial regulator; administers the Payment Services Act (MPI licences for stablecoins); initiator of Project Guardian tokenization initiative

participants.name: ACRA
participants.role: Corporate registry and accounting standards; oversees entity registration for tokenization SPVs and digital asset companies incorporated in Singapore

layers.label: Financial Institutions
layers.description: Major Singapore banks and financial institutions active in Project Guardian tokenization pilots and digital asset initiatives.

participants.name: DBS Bank
participants.role: Largest Singapore bank; Project Guardian founding participant; issued SGD stablecoin pilot; active in tokenized bond and fund settlement trials

participants.name: UOB
participants.role: Project Guardian participant; exploring tokenized trade finance and cross-border payments via Partior network

participants.name: OCBC
participants.role: Active in tokenized bond pilots; exploring digital asset custody and cross-border settlement use cases

participants.name: Standard Chartered (Libeara)
participants.role: Libeara is Standard Chartered's tokenization platform; issued tokenized Singapore government securities; Project Guardian participant

layers.label: Infrastructure
layers.description: Core infrastructure for Singapore's tokenized asset ecosystem: MAS Project Guardian settlement network, Partior interbank payment blockchain, and regulated digital securities exchange.

participants.name: Project Guardian
participants.role: MAS-led industry initiative for tokenization of financial assets; coordinates pilots across banks, asset managers, and DeFi protocols; launched 2022

participants.name: Partior
participants.role: Blockchain-based interbank settlement network founded by DBS, JPMorgan, and Standard Chartered; enables real-time multi-currency cross-border payment settlement

participants.name: ADDX (iSTOX)
participants.role: MAS-regulated private markets exchange; blockchain-based issuance, custody, and exchange for tokenized private market assets

layers.label: Issuance Platforms
layers.description: Platforms enabling tokenization of funds, carbon credits, and trade finance instruments with Singapore regulatory nexus or issued by MAS-regulated entities.

participants.name: BENJI
participants.full_name: Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund (BENJI)
participants.role: Tokenized U.S. government money fund by Franklin Templeton; Project Guardian participant in Singapore; RARM-assessed

participants.name: Climate Impact X
participants.role: Singapore-based global marketplace and exchange for high-quality carbon credits; explores tokenized carbon credit issuance and settlement

participants.name: Contour Network
participants.role: Blockchain-based trade finance network enabling tokenized letters of credit and trade document digitisation; Singapore-headquartered

layers.label: Market Access
layers.description: MAS-licensed digital securities exchanges and broker-dealers providing accredited and institutional investor access to tokenized assets in Singapore.

participants.name: ADDX Exchange
participants.role: MAS-regulated private market exchange; provides accredited and institutional investor access to tokenized bonds, private equity, and funds

participants.name: Phillip Securities
participants.role: MAS-licensed capital markets services firm; exploring tokenized securities distribution for retail and institutional clients

layers.participants_note: MAS uses Major Payment Institution (MPI) and Capital Markets Services (CMS) licence categories rather than a unified VATP framework. Participant counts reflect publicly disclosed MAS-licensed entities active in digital assets.

layers.label: Professional Enablers
layers.description: Digital asset custodians, compliance analytics providers, and professional service firms that underpin regulatory compliance and transaction assurance for the Singapore RWA ecosystem.

participants.name: Propine
participants.role: MAS-licensed digital asset custodian; institutional-grade custody for tokenized assets, digital securities, and private keys

participants.name: Sygnum
participants.role: Swiss-licensed digital asset bank with Singapore operations; offers digital asset banking, custody, and tokenization services to institutions

participants.name: Elliptic
participants.role: Crypto AML/KYC compliance platform; serves MAS-licensed exchanges and financial institutions in Singapore for transaction monitoring and sanctions screening

participants.name: Chainalysis
participants.role: Blockchain analytics and AML investigation platform; used by MAS-licensed virtual asset service providers for compliance and regulatory enforcement

layers.participants_note: Professional service providers listed are active in Singapore digital asset engagements based on public disclosures. This list is illustrative, not exhaustive.

gaps.title: No Local Digital Asset Credit Rating Agency
gaps.description: Singapore lacks a dedicated digital asset credit or quality rating agency. Risk assessment for tokenized assets relies on international firms without a Singapore-specific RWA methodology.

gaps.title: Retail Access Gap for Tokenized RWAs
gaps.description: MAS-licensed platforms primarily serve accredited and institutional investors. Retail investor access to tokenized real-world assets remains limited under existing regulatory frameworks.
